title: viola × juliae
intro: Viola × juliae is a hybrid species of the viola plant, resulting from the cross between Viola sororia and Viola labradorica. It is native to North America, with medicinal properties attributed to its leaves, stems, and roots, containing compounds such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides. The plant is valued for its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia, providing health benefits related to improved sleep quality and stress management. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of Viola × juliae are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
